privacyAndDataCard.manageDevicesText: See which devices are signed in. Use the button below to log out of selected devices, or log out of all devices using the link.
privacyAndDataCard.deleteAccountText: To permanently delete your Thunderbird Pro account and unsubscribe from the service, please reach out to our support team who will be able to assist you. Once your account is deleted, you will not be able to reactivate it or access your data.

yourCurrentSubscription.portalLinkError: Failed to get subscription portal link
yourCurrentSubscription.footerText: You’ll be taken to Paddle, our secure payment provider.

notFoundMessage.textAuth: Please {link} to go back to your dashboard.

serverErrorMessage.text: Please visit our {link} for more information.

getStartedWithThundermail.title: Get started with Thundermail
getStartedWithThundermail.description: Connect your new email address to start sending and receiving.

desktopPanel.connectDescription: Launch the configuration wizard to add Thundermail to desktop automatically.
desktopPanel.downloadDescription: We recommend using the desktop application for the best experience.

mobilePanel.qrCodeDescription: Go to Settings in Thunderbird for Android, add an account, and scan the QR code below.
mobilePanel.qrCodeAlt: A QR Code to be used with Thunderbird for Android account importing.
mobilePanel.downloadDescription: Install the mobile app on your phone or tablet.

otherAppsPanel.title: Automatic Configuration
otherAppsPanel.descriptionOne: Most modern email applications will detect your account settings automatically. Just enter your email and password.
otherAppsPanel.descriptionTwo: Some email apps may require an {appPassword} instead of your regular account sign-in.
otherAppsPanel.needHelpDescription: Read our step-by-step guide for connecting popular email applications.

dashboard.whatIsSmtpContent: SMTP stands for Simple Mail Transfer Protocol. It enables an email client (such as Thunderbird) to deliver messages to other email addresses through a mail server. For more information, {supportUrl}.
dashboard.whatIsImapContent: IMAP stands for Internet Message Access Protocol. It allows an email client (such as Thunderbird) to access messages stored on a mail server. For more information, {supportUrl}.
dashboard.whatIsJmapContent: JMAP stands for JSON Meta Application Protocol. It’s a robust way to receive and relay mail server messages in modern web-based or mobile environments. For more information, {supportUrl}.

emailSettings.changePasswordDescription: Use this separate password to connect apps that can't use your regular Thundermail sign-in.
emailSettings.changePasswordDescriptionTwo: Misplaced it? Create a new one.
emailSettings.createPasswordDescriptionTwo: Once set, it can't be viewed or recovered.
emailSettings.createPasswordTooltip: For security reasons, this password is never stored or shown again. For more information, {supportUrl}.
emailSettings.changePasswordTooltip: Changing your app password will log you out of all current email clients.
emailSettings.emailAliasesDescription: All emails will deliver mail to your primary email mailbox.
emailSettings.emailAliasesPrimaryChangeWarning: Changing your primary email may affect app sign-ins.
emailSettings.catchAllTooltip: Tool tip: All available domains already have a catch-all email. No additional catch-all aliases can be created. {supportUrl}
emailSettings.name: Name
emailSettings.requiredFieldsNotSet: Name and domain fields are required
emailSettings.nameHelp: {'Use 3-40 characters. You can use a-z, 0-9 and _(underscore).'}
emailSettings.nameCatchAllHelp: Leave empty or enter * to setup a catch-all address
emailSettings.nameValidationErrorPattern: You can only use a-z, 0-9 and _(underscore).
emailSettings.anErrorOccurredWhileChangingPassword: An error occurred while changing the password
emailSettings.anErrorOccurredWhileChangingDisplayName: An error occurred while changing the display name

customDomains.customDomainsDescriptionInitial: Use your own domain to create personalized email addresses.
customDomains.customDomainsDescriptionAdd: Adding a domain you own to your account allows you to create aliases using your custom domains.
customDomains.domainHelp: Enter your existing domain from registrars like GoDaddy, Namecheap, Google Domains or another registrar.
customDomains.verifyStepTitle: Next Step: Add your Thundermail DNS records
customDomains.verifyStepOneTitle: 1. Sign in to your domain registrar
customDomains.verifyStepOne: Log in where you manage your domain (for example, GoDaddy, Namecheap, or Google Domains) and open the DNS settings for your domain.
customDomains.verifyStepTwo: Delete any existing MX, TXT and SRV records related to email. This ensures your domain is ready to route mail through Thundermail.
customDomains.verifyStepThree: Add the following MX, TXT, and SRV records to your domain’s DNS settings. Enter each record exactly as shown below.
customDomains.verifyStepNote: {'If your registrar doesn’t allow "@" for the root domain, leave the Name/Host field empty.'}
customDomains.verifyStepInfoDescription: Once you start verification, we’ll keep trying automatically for a few days. If it doesn’t succeed in that time, we’ll stop and you’ll need to restart the process manually.
customDomains.domainAlreadyConfigured: This domain is already configured. Please {link} in case you believe this is a mistake.

verificationCriticalErrors.mxLookupError: Unable to find mail servers for domain
verificationCriticalErrors.connectionError: Failed to connect to mail server
verificationCriticalErrors.readGreetingError: We could not verify that the domain records connect to Thundermail

securitySettings.accountActivityDescriptionOne: Many email apps can configure your account automatically. Enter your email address and password to get started.
securitySettings.accountActivityDescriptionTwo: If setup doesn’t work, try connecting manually using server settings.
securitySettings.allowListDescription: Ensure important senders aren’t marked as spam. Emails on your allow list skip junk filtering, but may still be rejected if they’re flagged or improperly formatted.

securitySettings.accountActivityDescription: Recent devices signed in to your account.
securitySettings.noRecentDevices: No recent devices signed in to your account.
securitySettings.allowListDescription: Ensure important senders aren’t marked as spam. Emails on your allow list skip junk filtering, but may still be rejected if they’re flagged or improperly formatted.

step1.title: Create an account
step1.subtitle: Choose your Thundermail username.
step1.usernameHelp: This will become your email. e.g. yourname{domain}
step1.unknownError: There was a problem with your username. Please try another one.

step2.title: Set a password
step2.passwordHelp: Use a strong password you haven’t used elsewhere.
step2.yourNameHelp: Set the name people will see when you send emails.
step2.confirmPasswordDoesNotMatchError: Password and confirm password must match.

step3.title: Add a verification email
step3.subtitle: Use an email address you already have access to. It must be different from your new Thundermail address.
step3.verificationEmailHelp: Thundermail will send a verification link to this address to confirm it belongs to you.

step4.title: Check your email
step4.subtitle: In a few moments you should receive an email with instructions on how to activate your account.

ftue.initialWelcomeDescription: Would you like a quick tour of the dashboard?
ftue.step1Text: Connect your new email address to start sending and receiving messages. Use Thunderbird or any email app you prefer.
ftue.step2Text: Create an app password for email, calendar, and other apps that need a separate sign-in.
ftue.step3Text: Claim any additional email aliases you would like to use.
ftue.step4Text: Add custom domains to create email aliases for any need.
ftue.step5Text: Go to your Thunderbird Pro account to view account details, manage billing, or cancel your service.

manageMfa.multiFactorAuthenticationDescription: Multi-factor authentication (MFA) adds an extra layer of security by requiring more than a password to sign in. We recommend setting up at least two methods to avoid lockout. You can change or remove MFA methods anytime on this screen.

description.set: One-time codes are generated by your authenticator app.
description.notSet: Set an authenticator app to add an extra layer of security to your account.

description.set: One-time codes will be sent to {phoneNumber}. SMS recovery is enabled.
description.notSet: Set a recovery phone to avoid being locked out of your account.

description.set: One-time codes will be sent to {emailAddress}. Email recovery is enabled.
description.notSet: Set a recovery email address to avoid being locked out of your account.

verifyYourIdentity.title: Verify your identity
verifyYourIdentity.description: To keep your account secure, please verify your identity before making changes to sensitive information.
verifyYourIdentity.authenticatorAppDescription: Use your authenticator app to generate and enter a recovery code.
verifyYourIdentity.authenticatorAppCodeDescription: Enter the code from your authenticator app:
verifyYourIdentity.recoveryEmailAddressCodeDescription: Enter the code from your email:

manageAuthenticatorApp.title: Manage Authenticator App
manageAuthenticatorApp.description: Open your authenticator app and scan the QR code to link it to your account.
manageAuthenticatorApp.descriptionTwo: This device will be remembered, so next time you sign in, you won’t need to scan again. Just enter the one-time code from your app.
manageAuthenticatorApp.qrNote: Setting up a new authenticator app will remove your current one.
manageAuthenticatorApp.authenticatorAppCodeDescription: Enter the code from your authenticator app:

manageEmailRecovery.title: Manage Email Recovery
manageEmailRecovery.description: Enter your email to receive an authentication code. Once you get a message with the code, select continue.

privacyAndData.deleteAccountDataText: This will permanently delete your Thunderbird Pro account and all data. You cannot reactivate your account.
privacyAndData.deleteAccountWill: Deleting your Thunderbird Pro account will:
privacyAndData.deleteAccountWillOne: Permanently erase your account and all data (emails, contacts, attachments, events, passwords, files).
privacyAndData.deleteAccountWillTwo: Log you out of all devices and end access to Thunderbird Pro services.
privacyAndData.deleteAccountImportantOne: Deleting your data does not cancel your subscription. Unsubscribe first to stop billing and service access.
privacyAndData.deleteAccountImportantTwo: After X days, all data will be erased and your Thundermail address may be reassigned. Emails sent during this time will be rejected.
privacyAndData.deleteAccountExport: Export anything you want to keep before continuing.
privacyAndData.deleteAccountCheckbox: I understand my data will be permanently deleted

subscribe.paddleUnknownError: We're experiencing connectivity issues with our payment provider. Please try again later.
subscribe.planSystemError: No plan exists or the plan is incorrectly setup. Please correct this in the admin panel.
subscribe.paymentComplete: Please wait while we verify payment, you will be redirected shortly.
subscribe.title: Order Summary

verification.title: Your payment is being processed.
verification.text: This step can take up to 48 hours to complete.

contact.title: Submit a request
contact.requiredFieldsText: Fields marked with an asterisk (*) are required.
contact.errorFetchingFields: Failed to fetch ticket fields. Please reload the page and try again.
contact.errorSubmittingForm: Failed to submit contact form. Please try again.
contact.success: Your support request has been submitted successfully
contact.dragAndDropFilesText: Drag & drop files here, or click to upload
contact.filesSelectedText: {count} file(s) selected. Drag & drop more files here, or click to upload
